title: Earnings call transcript: Power Integrations beats Q1 2026 forecasts but stock dips
text: Power Integrations reported Q1 2026 earnings of $0.25 per diluted share, beating analyst estimates of $0.23. Revenue came in at $108.3 million versus analyst estimates of $106.6 million, representing a 1.6% beat. Adjusted operating income came in at $12.65 million versus analyst estimates of $11 million, a 15% beat. Despite beating expectations on earnings and margins, the stock dipped following the earnings announcement, highlighting investor concerns about forward guidance and market conditions. Q2 2026 guidance for revenue of $115-120 million at the midpoint was 2.2% above analyst consensus estimates, suggesting management's optimistic outlook for near-term momentum. The industrial segment continues to be the strongest performer, with 23% year-over-year growth. Management commentary focused on three strategic priorities: customer alignment in product development, product pipeline streamlining for faster time-to-market, and operational efficiency improvements. New leadership in worldwide sales, with the appointment of Mike Balow from onsemi, signals strengthened focus on automotive and industrial markets.
